name: Phaze II
notes: Reliable benchmark shape for reading the lane and building the rest of an arsenal around.

name: Black Widow 3.0
notes: Big asymmetric option when the front part of the lane has volume and you need the ball to dig.

name: Attention Star
notes: Angular move for when solids start reading too early and you need shape downlane.

name: Venom Shock
notes: Control piece for flatter looks wet-dry house shots or bowlers who need blend.

name: Harsh Reality
notes: Top-end oil ball for tournament volume longer patterns and speed-dominant bowlers.

name: IQ Tour Ruby
notes: Clean responsive pearl for moving left or opening the lane when benchmark pieces get lazy.

name: Purple Pearl Urethane
notes: Short-pattern or control option; not a default house-shot purchase unless the bowler has a clear use case.

name: TZone
notes: Straight spare ball. Every serious arsenal should have one if corner pins are costing score.

name: Phaze AI
notes: TX-16 pearl benchmark option with Power Edge finish for strong backend motion.

name: Ion Max
notes: Top-end Storm asymmetric solid for fresh oil and bowlers needing stronger midlane traction.

name: Rock Star
notes: NanoStar solid reactive with Rocker A.I. core for medium-heavy oil and a strong symmetric read.

name: Full Effect
notes: Effect-line pearl for midlane strength with controllable backend shape between stronger and cleaner Hammer options.

name: Black Widow Mania
notes: Strong Black Widow shape for fresh oil or carrydown when the bowler needs traction plus continuation.

name: Ethos Hybrid
notes: Brunswick asymmetric hybrid for a strong read with more downlane shape than a solid.

name: Crimson Jackal
notes: High-end Motiv asymmetric option for heavy oil and speed-dominant players who need early traction.

name: Subzero Forge
notes: Strong symmetric Forge shape for medium-heavy volume when the bowler wants traction without big asymmetric flare.

name: Hyper Venom
notes: Cleaner pearl Venom shape for transition and medium oil when control pieces are too early.

name: Double Trouble
notes: DV8 no-thumb focused pearl that blends transition and gives rev-dominant bowlers a controlled backend window.

name: Hater Pearl
notes: Strong shiny DV8 asym for bowlers who need a cleaner complement to heavy-oil solids without losing midlane read.

name: No Doubt Solid
notes: Super-symmetric Radical solid for a strong versatile slot between benchmark solids and high-end asymmetric pieces.

name: Theorem Pearl
notes: Track asymmetric pearl that pushes the breakpoint farther downlane than the original Theorem.

name: Atlas Hybrid
notes: Big asymmetric hybrid option with cleaner length and strong backend shape for medium-heavy lane volume.

name: Crusher Hybrid
notes: Ebonite asymmetric hybrid for bowlers needing length and a stronger backend response on medium-heavy oil.

name: Game Breaker 5 Hybrid
notes: Value benchmark hybrid that keeps the Game Breaker read while adding a cleaner look through the fronts.
